     106              : 
     107              : bcm_infallible bssl::BCM_sha256_final(uint8_t out[SHA256_DIGEST_LENGTH],
     108       160561 :                                       SHA256_CTX *c) {
     109              :   // Ideally we would assert `sha->md_len` is `SHA256_DIGEST_LENGTH` tomatch the
     110              :   // size hint, but calling code often pairs `SHA224_Init` with `SHA256_Final`
     111              :   // and expects `sha->md_len` to carry the size over.
     112              :   //
     113              :   // TODO(davidben): Add an assert and fix code to match them up.
     114       160561 :   sha256_final_impl(out, c->md_len, c);
     115       160561 :   return bcm_infallible::approved;
     116       160561 : }
     117              : 
     118              : bcm_infallible bssl::BCM_sha224_final(uint8_t out[SHA224_DIGEST_LENGTH],
     119            0 :                                       SHA256_CTX *ctx) {
     120              :   // This function must be paired with `SHA224_Init`, which sets `ctx->md_len`
     121              :   // to `SHA224_DIGEST_LENGTH`.
     122            0 :   assert(ctx->md_len == SHA224_DIGEST_LENGTH);
     123            0 :   sha256_final_impl(out, SHA224_DIGEST_LENGTH, ctx);
     124            0 :   return bcm_infallible::approved;

     145              : // See FIPS 180-4, section 4.1.2.
     146              : #define Sigma0(x)                                       \
     147     10273808 :   (CRYPTO_rotr_u32((x), 2) ^ CRYPTO_rotr_u32((x), 13) ^ \

     154              : #define sigma1(x) \
     155      7704720 :   (CRYPTO_rotr_u32((x), 17) ^ CRYPTO_rotr_u32((x), 19) ^ ((x) >> 10))
     156              : 
     157     10273808 : #define Ch(x, y, z) (((x) & (y)) ^ ((~(x)) & (z)))
     158     10273808 : #define Maj(x, y, z) (((x) & (y)) ^ ((x) & (z)) ^ ((y) & (z)))
     159              : 
     160              : #define ROUND_00_15(i, a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h)   \
     161     10273808 :   do {                                           \
     162     10273808 :     T1 += h + Sigma1(e) + Ch(e, f, g) + K256[i]; \
     163     10273808 :     h = Sigma0(a) + Maj(a, b, c);                \
     164     10273808 :     d += T1;                                     \
     165     10273808 :     h += T1;                                     \
     166     10273808 :   } while (0)
     167              : 
     168              : #define ROUND_16_63(i, a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, X)      \

     170      7704720 :     s0 = X[(i + 1) & 0x0f];                            \
     171      7704720 :     s0 = sigma0(s0);                                   \
     172      7704720 :     s1 = X[(i + 14) & 0x0f];                           \
     173      7704720 :     s1 = sigma1(s1);                                   \
     174      7704720 :     T1 = X[(i) & 0x0f] += s0 + s1 + X[(i + 9) & 0x0f]; \

     176      7704720 :   } while (0)
